element_introduction_order.mastery_context: movement points management across hexagonal grid
gold and mana generation, city upgrading
managing up to 8 units per tile, morale dynamics
diplomacy, city revolts, unit desertion mechanics
skill point allocation up to level 30, experience farming
hero spell-casting specialization levels I-V, channeling points
hiring wandering units, race disposition management
500 mana cost, large-scale tactical weapon deployment
silver/gold medal thresholds, level-dependent experience scaling
gold per turn maintenance, summoned unit mana upkeep

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based strategy with variable pacing: early game emphasizes expansion and exploration (safe relief cycles), mid-game introduces military conflicts and hero management (moderate tension oscillation), late-game focuses on large-scale warfare and altar deployment (sustained high tension)

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Scattered independent unit groups and altars across map represent optional encounters and power sources; player chooses engagement order
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Altar structures function as high-value territorial control points requiring 500 mana to activate as area weapons

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-oriented with basic unit movement, city management, and resource gathering establishing foundational mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of hero recruitment, spell casting, race diplomacy, and independent unit hiring creating multi-layered strategic decision-making
difficulty_curve.late_game: Large-scale military campaigns, altar warfare, complex race relations, and high-level hero customization creating endgame complexity
notable_spikes: First major enemy encounter requiring tactical combat understanding
Mid-game transition to hero-dependent spell casting
Altar availability and large-scale area damage introduction

notable_level_patterns.description: Player actions queue sequentially or simultaneously depending on game mode; each turn regenerates movement points, accumulates mana (50 per turn at altars), and requires unit upkeep payment, creating cyclical economic tension

Goodwill acts (city upgrades) improve race relations; hostile acts (razing, looting) worsen them, manifesting in city revolts, unit desertion, and recruitment refusal—spatial expansion must account for ideological compatibility

Heroes serve as rare random spawns that unlock spell-casting potential; player must capture them, level them through combat, and invest skill points strategically—creates variable power availability across playthroughs

Up to 12 players on single maps share hexagonal grid; sequential turn order creates diplomatic/military tension windows; simultaneous movement only available in single-player/live multiplayer (not PBEM)
